metadata.title: Guideline on the clinical investigation of anti-anginal medicinal products in stable angina pectoris

rules.rule_text: The primary efficacy criterion for antianginal drugs is the assessment of total exercise capacity at trough (end of dose interval).
rules.verbatim_quote: The assessment of total exercise capacity at trough which is defined as the residual effect at the end of the dose interval, is the primary efficacy criterion for antianginal drugs.

rules.rule_text: Total exercise capacity (total exercise time or total workload) should be the primary endpoint in therapeutic clinical trials.
rules.verbatim_quote: Total exercise capacity (total exercise time or total workload performed) is probably the most meaningful measurement that can be obtained from exercise testing, and thus it should be considered as the primary endpoint in therapeutic clinical trials.

rules.rule_text: Other exercise variables (time to onset of angina, time to 1mm ST depression) must show consistency with total exercise capacity; a consistent result in these variables should be demonstrated before accepting an effect on exercise capacity.
rules.verbatim_quote: other exercise variables should be used for an adequate characterisation of the effect on antianginal effect in addition to total exercise capacity. A consistent result in these exercise variables should be demonstrated before accepting an effect on exercise capacity.

rules.rule_text: All three exercise variables (total exercise capacity, time to onset of angina, time to ST segment depression) should be measured in therapeutic trials and must show consistency or at least no major contradiction.
rules.verbatim_quote: The above three variables can be considered to be relevant to therapeutic effect, and all of them should be measured in therapeutic trials. They must show consistency or at least no major contradiction between them in order to conclude efficacy of a new drug.

rules.rule_text: Anginal pain frequency, intensity, and duration is not considered a primary endpoint. It is relevant as a secondary endpoint only.
rules.source_section: 3.2 Anginal pain / consumption of short acting nitrates
rules.verbatim_quote: Frequency, intensity and duration of anginal pain should be documented. Whereas this variable is not considered to be a primary endpoint, it is relevant as a secondary endpoint in both short-term and long-term studies.

rules.rule_text: Short-acting nitrate consumption is of limited clinical value and should only be considered a secondary endpoint.
rules.source_section: 3.2 Anginal pain / consumption of short acting nitrates
rules.verbatim_quote: The concomitant use of short acting nitrates has been classically accepted as a supportive clinical measurement. This parameter is highly variable and today is considered of limited clinical value and should only be considered a secondary end point

rules.rule_text: Clinical evidence of symptomatic improvement in terms of anginal pain and HRQoL should also be provided in the major therapeutic trials.
rules.verbatim_quote: In addition, clinical evidence of symptomatic improvement in terms of anginal pain and HRQoL (Health-Related Quality of Life) should also be provided in the major therapeutic trials.

rules.rule_text: Effects on cardiovascular and total morbidity and mortality should be evaluated as a relevant safety parameter, especially if there is suspicion of detrimental effects.
rules.verbatim_quote: However, effects on cardiovascular and total morbidity and mortality should be evaluated as a relevant safety parameter. This should be done in particular if there is a reasoned suspicion that a new drug might have detrimental effects on these parameters.
rules.exceptions: No requirement to prove beneficial effect on morbidity/mortality for marketing authorisation unless specifically claimed.

rules.rule_text: Exercise protocols must be validated and follow recommendations of international organisations of cardiology. ST segment abnormality characterisation must be based on internationally accepted criteria.
rules.verbatim_quote: The exercise protocols should be validated and follow the recommendations of international organisations of cardiology. The characterisation of ST segment abnormalities suggesting ischaemia should be based on internationally accepted criteria.

rules.rule_text: At least two standardised exercise tests must be performed at study start. The difference between the tests should not exceed 20%. If patients are included with a larger difference, a rationale must be given.
rules.verbatim_quote: At least two standardised exercise tests should be performed at the start of the study. The difference between the tests should not exceed 20 %. If patients are included with a larger difference a rationale for this should be given.
rules.exceptions: Patients with >20% difference can be included if rationale is provided.

rules.rule_text: Total exercise capacity as the main outcome measure is expected to be expressed in METs change from baseline.
rules.verbatim_quote: it is expected that, as the main outcome measure, total exercise capacity is expressed in METS change from baseline.

rules.rule_text: Exercise tests must be performed under medical supervision in facilities equipped to treat any cardiac complication.
rules.verbatim_quote: The physiological testing of the patients must be performed under medical supervision in facilities equipped to treat any cardiac complication.

rules.rule_text: Exercise tests must be performed and show difference/benefit at trough levels of the drug. Differences at peak level may also be included.
rules.verbatim_quote: Exercise tests are expected to be performed and difference / benefit shown at trough levels (of the drug). Differences at peak level may also be included.

rules.rule_text: Anginal pain must be recorded in a patient diary along with concomitant use of short-acting nitrates. Daily frequency, severity, and duration should be registered using log books.
rules.verbatim_quote: The patient's experience of anginal pain should be recorded in a patient diary as well as the concomitant use of short-acting nitrates. The daily frequency, severity and duration, of anginal pain should whenever possible be registered by patients using available log books.

rules.rule_text: HRQoL questionnaires must be validated, clinically responsive, and capable of differentiating clinically important improvement or deterioration from random or non-specific changes.
rules.verbatim_quote: Validated questionnaires should be shown to be clinically responsive and capable of differentiating clinically important improvement or deterioration from random or non-specific changes.

rules.rule_text: Cardiovascular morbidity and mortality must always be evaluated and assessed using pooled data of the controlled trials.
rules.verbatim_quote: Cardiovascular morbidity and mortality can be measured in specially designed studies, if specifically claimed, but should always be evaluated and assessed using the pooled data of the (controlled) trials.

rules.rule_text: Patients must have stable angina pectoris documented by coronary heart disease evidence (proven MI, previous coronary revascularisation, and/or coronary angiography). For dose-finding studies, documentation of unequivocal coronary heart disease is mandatory.
rules.verbatim_quote: The patients included in the studies must suffer from stable angina pectoris on the basis of coronary heart disease, preferably documented by a history of proven myocardial infarction, previous coronary revascularisation and/or coronary angiography. With regard to dose-finding studies the documentation of unequivocal coronary heart disease is mandatory.

rules.rule_text: Post-revascularisation patients may only be included at least 6 months after the procedure. Post-MI patients should be included only after stable angina for more than 30 days.
rules.verbatim_quote: these patients should only be included at least 6 months after the revascularisation procedure. Similarly, patients with stable angina more than 30 days after MI should be adequately represented.

rules.rule_text: Women and elderly patients must be sufficiently represented in the study population.
rules.verbatim_quote: Women and elderly patients should be sufficiently represented in the study population. Efficacy and safety data in these patient populations should be consistent with the overall benefit/risk evaluation.

rules.rule_text: Only patients with an interpretable baseline ECG at rest should be studied. Patients must be capable of exercise testing according to standardised protocols.
rules.verbatim_quote: Since for confirmatory pivotal studies the primary efficacy end-point is total exercise capacity, patients to be included in the study must be capable of exercise testing according to standardised protocols. Only patients with an interpretable baseline ECG at rest should be studied.

rules.rule_text: Symptoms of angina pectoris and nitrate consumption must have been stable at least during the 2 weeks preceding the study. Concomitant medication should remain unchanged during the study.
rules.verbatim_quote: The symptoms of angina pectoris and the nitrate consumption must have been stable at least during the 2 weeks preceding the study. Any concomitant medication should remain unchanged during the study.

rules.rule_text: Dose-response studies must be randomised, placebo-controlled, double-blinded, using at least three dosages, designed as parallel group studies lasting at least six weeks.
rules.verbatim_quote: Dose-response studies should be randomised, placebo-controlled and double-blinded using at least three dosages to establish the clinically useful dose range as well as the optimal dose. These studies should preferably be designed as parallel group studies and should last at least six weeks.

rules.rule_text: Main therapeutic studies must be randomised, double blind, controlled parallel group design, at least 12 weeks duration, with active comparator adequately dosed.
rules.verbatim_quote: These studies should be performed in a randomised, double blind, controlled parallel group design. Active control studies with an active comparator adequately dosed, of at least 12 weeks duration, should demonstrate comparable efficacy and safety to an appropriate standard therapy.

rules.rule_text: A sufficient number of patients must be treated for a minimum of 6 months or one year for long-term safety. Pro-anginal and pro-arrhythmic effects, withdrawal phenomena, and rebound effects must be specifically studied.
rules.verbatim_quote: A sufficient number of patients should be treated for a minimum of 6 months or for one year, preferably in controlled trials. All adverse events occurring during the clinical trials as well as morbidity and mortality data must be fully described, assessed and discussed in the context of current knowledge about antianginal drug therapy.
rules.exceptions: For novel mechanisms of action (e.g., angiogenesis), longer observation periods (minimum 1-year) will be required.
